pattern: Every prefix and suffix of the number is itself a small integer, so the whole check collapses to running an ordinary primality test on at most twenty short numbers derived from the digit string.
hints: Convert the number to its digit string once; every prefix and suffix you need is just a slice of that string turned back into an integer.

You need a plain primality test (trial division up to the square root is more than fast enough for numbers this size) and you need to apply it to every prefix and every suffix, not just the number itself.
Stop and return false the moment any single prefix or suffix fails the primality test -- there is no need to check the rest once one candidate fails.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, all prefixes and suffixes prime
the number itself is prime but an inner prefix is not
a longer number where every prefix and suffix happens to be prime

stress.description: random numbers across the full allowed range
